name: Kamatera Cloud API
what_it_does: Enterprise cloud infrastructure with REST API — enabling businesses to provision cloud servers, load balancers, private networks, and block storage across 20+ global data centers including unusual locations (Israel, UAE, Hong Kong, Canada, Netherlands), with hourly billing, high-performance SSDs, and a developer REST API for infrastructure automation.
use_cases: Provisioning Kamatera cloud servers via API for automated infrastructure deployment
Managing Kamatera load balancers and private networks via API for infrastructure automation
Scaling Kamatera server capacity via API for automated resource management
Provisioning Kamatera servers in specific global regions via API for geo-targeted infrastructure
Managing Kamatera firewall rules via API for network security automation
not_for: Developer-friendly PaaS (Kamatera is IaaS — use Railway or Render for PaaS)
Serverless or managed services (Kamatera is raw compute)
Teams needing extensive documentation and community resources
best_when: A business needs cloud compute in specific global regions not served by major clouds (Israel, UAE, Middle East, etc.) — or needs high-performance bare-metal VPS at competitive hourly pricing.
avoid_when: You need managed services, developer PaaS, extensive documentation, or are outside the regions where Kamatera has location advantages.

auth.notes: Client ID + Secret from Kamatera console. Bearer token obtained via token endpoint. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes — full account access. No webhooks. REST API with token-based auth. API documentation in Kamatera developer console. No SDKs officially provided.

pricing.paid_tiers: Hourly billing from $4/month (1vCPU, 1GB RAM); pay-as-you-go; enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Israeli company (Petah Tikva). Founded 1995 by Maxim Kats. Subsidiary of Cloudwatt Group. 20+ global data centers: US (East/West), UK, Germany, Netherlands, Italy, Israel, UAE, Hong Kong, Canada, Singapore, and more. Known for availability in unusual regions (Israel, UAE) not covered by major clouds. 24/7 support. SSD storage. Windows and Linux. Hourly billing — granular cost control. $30 credit on trial. Used by enterprises needing specific geo locations.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with token auth. No webhooks. No SDKs. Limited public documentation. No fine-grained scopes. Israeli company with Middle East data center advantages. Rate limits not documented. IaaS-only — no managed services.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Server reads idempotent by server ID. Network reads idempotent. Server creation not idempotent — check by name. Power state operations idempotent for same desired state.
known_agent_gotchas: Limited documentation — API details mostly in console developer section
No webhooks — must poll for server task completion
No SDKs — must implement REST client from scratch
Israeli origin — evaluate regulatory exposure for some workloads
Rate limits not documented — test before automation

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. ISO27001 certified. Bearer token auth. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. No fine-grained access controls. Israeli jurisdiction — evaluate data residency requirements. Multiple global regions including IL and AE. 24/7 security support. No compliance certifications beyond ISO27001 documented.
